zh.title: 阿里开源 AI 沙箱 OpenSandbox
zh.subtitle: 生产级代码隔离执行方案，自托管替代 E2B 的新选择
zh.tldr: 阿里巴巴开源 OpenSandbox，提供多语言 SDK 和 Docker/K8s 运行时，支持 Coding Agent、GUI Agent 等场景的安全代码执行，单日 1179 star，是 E2B 的可自托管替代方案。

keyMetrics.subject: OpenSandbox

rows.note: E2B 基于 Firecracker 微虚拟机
自托管模式无时间限制
OpenSandbox 为自托管方案，仅承担基础设施成本
OpenSandbox 支持基于域名的细粒度出口控制

keyMetrics.footnote: 价格数据来源于 Superagent 2026 年 AI 沙箱基准测试报告，OpenSandbox 为自托管开源方案无 SaaS 定价

zh.whatHappened:
阿里巴巴于 2026 年 3 月初在 GitHub 开源了 **OpenSandbox**，一个通用 AI 应用沙箱平台，Apache 2.0 许可证：

- **单日获得 1179 star**，总星数迅速突破 3400+，显示市场对自托管 AI 沙箱方案的强烈需求
- 提供 **Python、Java/Kotlin、JavaScript/TypeScript、C#/.NET** 四种语言 SDK，覆盖主流开发生态
- 支持 **Docker 和 Kubernetes** 双运行时，控制面（FastAPI 生命周期服务器）与数据面（Go execd 执行守护进程）分离架构
- 内置命令行执行、文件系统操作、代码解释器（基于 Jupyter）、浏览器自动化（Chrome/Playwright）和 VS Code 桌面环境
- 提供 **FQDN 级别的网络出口策略控制**，通过 DNS 代理 + nftables 实现细粒度隔离
zh.whyItMatters:
AI Agent 安全执行代码是当前最关键的基础设施缺口之一，OpenSandbox 的出现改变了这个领域的竞争格局：

1. **自托管 vs SaaS 的选择权**：E2B 虽好但是托管服务，数据离开你的基础设施。OpenSandbox 让你在自己的 Docker/K8s 集群上运行，数据不出网
2. **生产级网络隔离**：不是简单的全有或全无网络策略，而是基于 FQDN 的细粒度出口控制——你可以允许沙箱访问 pypi.org 但禁止其他出站流量
3. **多语言 SDK 降低集成门槛**：Java/C# 开发者终于有了一手公民级别的 AI 沙箱 SDK，不需要再通过 REST API 手动对接
4. **控制面/数据面分离**：SDK 直接与沙箱内的 execd 通信（端口 44772），绕过服务器瓶颈，适合高并发场景

groups.name: AI Agent/Coding Agent 开发者
groups.description: 直接获得一个可自托管的生产级代码沙箱方案，替代或补充 E2B 等 SaaS 服务

groups.name: 平台工程师/DevOps
groups.description: 需要评估是否在现有 K8s 集群上部署 OpenSandbox 以支持团队的 AI 代码执行需求

groups.name: 个人开发者/独立开发者
groups.description: 可以在本地 Docker 上零成本搭建 AI 沙箱环境，不再受 E2B 免费额度限制

groups.name: 企业安全/合规团队
groups.description: 自托管方案满足数据不出网的合规要求，FQDN 级网络策略提供可审计的安全边界

immediate: 在本地用 Docker 跑通 OpenSandbox 的 Quick Start：`uv pip install opensandbox-server && opensandbox-server init-config ~/.sandbox.toml --example docker && opensandbox-server`，预计 15 分钟
用 Python SDK 测试一个基础工作流：创建沙箱 → 执行 shell 命令 → 运行代码解释器 → 销毁沙箱，验证端到端体验
thisWeek: 对比 OpenSandbox 和你当前使用的沙箱方案（E2B/自建 Docker/无沙箱），从冷启动时间、网络隔离能力、SDK 易用性三个维度评估
如果你的项目使用 Java 或 C#，重点测试对应 SDK 的完整度——这是 OpenSandbox 相对竞品的独特优势
watchAndWait: 关注 Go SDK 的开发进度和 Kubernetes Helm Chart 的正式发布——这两项完成后才是生产部署的最佳时机
追踪社区反馈和 issue 情况，开源初期可能存在边界场景 bug，等 1-2 个月让社区打磨后再用于生产

options.name: 用 OpenSandbox 替换 E2B
options.description: 将 AI 代码执行环境从 E2B SaaS 迁移到自托管的 OpenSandbox
options.suitableFor: 对数据主权有要求的团队、E2B 免费额度不够用的个人开发者、需要 Java/C# SDK 的项目
options.cost: 需要运维自己的 Docker/K8s 集群，初期部署和调优约 1-2 天
options.risk: 项目刚开源，生产稳定性未经大规模验证；冷启动性能数据尚未公布

options.name: OpenSandbox 作为备选方案并行测试
options.description: 保留现有沙箱方案，同时在非生产环境部署 OpenSandbox 进行评估
options.suitableFor: 希望保持灵活性的团队，或正在自建沙箱方案的团队
options.cost: 评估测试约 1-2 天人力，基础设施成本可忽略
options.risk: 低风险，最多浪费几天评估时间

options.name: 保持现状
options.description: 继续使用当前的沙箱方案（E2B/Modal/自建），不做改变
options.suitableFor: 当前方案运行稳定且满足需求的团队，或不需要自托管的场景
options.risk: 如果后续需要自托管沙箱方案，可能错过跟进社区早期发展的机会；但目前没有紧迫切换需求

risks.title: 开源初期不等于生产就绪
risks.description: 3400 star 和阿里背书给人信心，但开源不到一周。K8s Helm Chart 还在开发中，Go SDK 在路线图上。现在就投入生产为时过早，建议等 1-2 个版本迭代后再评估生产可用性。

risks.title: 自托管的隐性运维成本
risks.description: 免费不等于零成本。自托管 OpenSandbox 需要管理 Docker/K8s 集群、监控沙箱生命周期、处理网络策略配置。如果你的团队没有 DevOps 能力，E2B 的 SaaS 模式反而更经济。

risks.title: 冷启动性能未经验证
risks.description: 竞品 E2B 的 ~150ms 和 Daytona 的 ~90ms 冷启动时间都有公开数据。OpenSandbox 尚未公布性能基准，基于 Docker/K8s 的架构在冷启动上可能不如 Firecracker 微虚拟机方案。

sources.title: alibaba/OpenSandbox — GitHub 官方仓库
sources.excerpt: 通用 AI 应用沙箱平台，提供多语言 SDK（Python/Java/JS/C#）、Docker/K8s 运行时、FQDN 级网络策略控制，Apache 2.0 许可证，3400+ star。

sources.title: alibaba/OpenSandbox 技术架构深度分析
sources.excerpt: 详细分析 OpenSandbox 的控制面/数据面分离架构、execd 执行守护进程、FQDN 级出口控制实现、SDK 设计模式和部署配置。

sources.title: OpenSandbox：AI 代理的沙盒
sources.excerpt: 分析 OpenSandbox 的核心功能、与 SmolVM 等竞品对比、K8s 原生支持的企业适配性优势。开源一周获 2000+ star。

sources.title: OpenSandbox — Alibaba's Open-Source AI Application Sandbox Platform
sources.excerpt: OpenSandbox 提供细粒度网络策略控制和资源隔离，支持 CPU/内存/磁盘/网络配额，集成 Seccomp 和 AppArmor 安全模块。

sources.title: AI Code Sandbox Benchmark 2026 — Modal vs E2B vs Daytona
sources.excerpt: 2026 年 AI 沙箱基准测试：E2B 冷启动 ~150ms/$0.083/h，Daytona ~90ms/$0.083/h，Modal 亚秒级/$0.119/h。OpenSandbox 作为自托管方案提供不同的价值主张。

en.title: Alibaba Open-Sources OpenSandbox for AI Agents
en.subtitle: Production-grade self-hosted sandbox — a real alternative to E2B
en.tldr: Alibaba released OpenSandbox under Apache 2.0, offering multi-language SDKs (Python/Java/JS/C#), Docker/K8s runtimes, and FQDN-level network policies for secure AI code execution. 1,179 stars on day one.

keyMetrics.subject: OpenSandbox

rows.note: Self-hosted mode has no time restrictions
OpenSandbox is self-hosted; you only pay for infra

keyMetrics.footnote: Pricing data from Superagent AI Sandbox Benchmark 2026. OpenSandbox is self-hosted with no SaaS pricing.

en.whatHappened:
Alibaba open-sourced **OpenSandbox** in early March 2026, a general-purpose sandbox platform for AI applications under the Apache 2.0 license:

- **1,179 stars on day one**, rapidly exceeding 3,400+ total — signaling strong demand for self-hosted AI sandbox solutions
- Ships with **Python, Java/Kotlin, JavaScript/TypeScript, and C#/.NET SDKs**, covering all major development ecosystems
- Supports **Docker and Kubernetes** runtimes with a clean control plane (FastAPI lifecycle server) / data plane (Go execd daemon) separation
- Built-in shell execution, filesystem operations, code interpreter (Jupyter-backed), browser automation (Chrome/Playwright), and VS Code desktop environment
- Provides **FQDN-level egress network policies** via DNS proxy + nftables for fine-grained sandbox isolation
en.whyItMatters:
Secure code execution for AI agents is one of the most critical infrastructure gaps today. OpenSandbox shifts the competitive landscape:

1. **Self-hosted vs SaaS optionality**: E2B works well but is a hosted service — your data leaves your infrastructure. OpenSandbox runs on your own Docker/K8s cluster with data staying in-network
2. **Production-grade network isolation**: Not binary allow/deny, but FQDN-based egress controls — allow pypi.org while blocking all other outbound traffic per sandbox
3. **Multi-language SDKs lower integration barriers**: Java and C# developers finally get first-class AI sandbox SDKs instead of manual REST API integration
4. **Control/data plane separation**: SDKs communicate directly with in-sandbox execd on port 44772, bypassing server bottlenecks for high-concurrency workloads

groups.name: AI Agent / Coding Agent Developers
groups.description: Gain a self-hosted production-grade sandbox option to replace or complement E2B and other SaaS services

groups.name: Platform Engineers / DevOps
groups.description: Need to evaluate deploying OpenSandbox on existing K8s clusters to support team AI code execution needs

groups.name: Individual Developers
groups.description: Can run AI sandboxes on local Docker at zero cost, no longer constrained by E2B's free tier limits

groups.name: Enterprise Security / Compliance Teams
groups.description: Self-hosted deployment meets data sovereignty requirements; FQDN-level policies provide auditable security boundaries

immediate: Run OpenSandbox Quick Start locally with Docker: `uv pip install opensandbox-server && opensandbox-server init-config ~/.sandbox.toml --example docker && opensandbox-server` — takes about 15 minutes
Test a basic workflow with the Python SDK: create sandbox, run shell commands, execute code interpreter, destroy sandbox — validate the end-to-end experience
thisWeek: Compare OpenSandbox against your current sandbox solution (E2B/self-built Docker/none) across cold start time, network isolation capabilities, and SDK usability
If your project uses Java or C#, focus-test those SDKs — this is OpenSandbox's unique advantage over competitors
watchAndWait: Track Go SDK development and Kubernetes Helm Chart GA release — production deployment is best after both land
Monitor community feedback and issue tracker; early open-source releases may have edge-case bugs — give it 1-2 months of community hardening before production use

options.name: Replace E2B with OpenSandbox
options.description: Migrate AI code execution from E2B SaaS to self-hosted OpenSandbox
options.suitableFor: Teams with data sovereignty requirements, developers hitting E2B free tier limits, projects needing Java/C# SDKs
options.cost: Requires maintaining your own Docker/K8s cluster; initial deployment and tuning takes 1-2 days
options.risk: Freshly open-sourced with production stability unproven at scale; cold start performance benchmarks not yet published

options.name: Parallel evaluation
options.description: Keep current sandbox solution while deploying OpenSandbox in non-production for evaluation
options.suitableFor: Teams wanting flexibility, or those currently building custom sandbox solutions
options.cost: 1-2 days of evaluation effort; infrastructure costs negligible
options.risk: Low risk — worst case is a few days spent evaluating

options.name: Do nothing
options.description: Continue with current sandbox setup (E2B/Modal/custom), make no changes
options.suitableFor: Teams with stable, working sandbox solutions that meet current requirements, or no need for self-hosting
options.risk: May miss early community development momentum if self-hosted sandbox becomes a future need; but no urgent reason to switch today

risks.title: Open source ≠ production ready on day one
risks.description: 3,400 stars and Alibaba's backing inspire confidence, but the project is less than a week old. K8s Helm Charts are still in development, Go SDK is on the roadmap. Deploying to production now is premature — wait for 1-2 release iterations before evaluating production readiness.

risks.title: Hidden ops cost of self-hosting
risks.description: Free doesn't mean zero cost. Self-hosting OpenSandbox requires managing Docker/K8s clusters, monitoring sandbox lifecycles, and configuring network policies. If your team lacks DevOps capacity, E2B's SaaS model may be more economical despite the per-hour pricing.

risks.title: Cold start performance is unverified
risks.description: Competitors publish benchmarks: E2B at ~150ms and Daytona at ~90ms cold starts. OpenSandbox has no published performance data yet. Its Docker/K8s architecture may not match Firecracker microVM cold start speeds.

sources.title: alibaba/OpenSandbox — Official GitHub Repository
sources.excerpt: General-purpose AI sandbox platform with multi-language SDKs (Python/Java/JS/C#), Docker/K8s runtimes, FQDN-level network policies. Apache 2.0 license, 3,400+ stars.

sources.title: alibaba/OpenSandbox Technical Architecture Deep Dive
sources.excerpt: Detailed analysis of OpenSandbox's control/data plane separation, execd daemon, FQDN egress control implementation, SDK patterns, and deployment configuration.

sources.title: OpenSandbox: The Sandbox for AI Agents
sources.excerpt: Analysis of OpenSandbox features, comparison with SmolVM, and Kubernetes-native enterprise suitability. 2,000+ stars within first week of open source.

sources.title: OpenSandbox — Alibaba's Open-Source AI Application Sandbox Platform
sources.excerpt: Fine-grained network policy control, resource isolation with CPU/memory/disk/network quotas, integrated Seccomp and AppArmor security modules.

sources.title: AI Code Sandbox Benchmark 2026 — Modal vs E2B vs Daytona
sources.excerpt: 2026 AI sandbox benchmarks: E2B cold start ~150ms at $0.083/h, Daytona ~90ms at $0.083/h, Modal sub-second at $0.119/h. OpenSandbox offers a different value proposition as self-hosted.
